question.text: Under which statute was FEDAI (Foreign Exchange Dealers' Association of India) incorporated when it was set up in 1958?

answer.text: Section 18A of the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Act, 1956
Section 10 of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text states: "Foreign Exchange Dealers' Association of India (FEDAI) was set up in 1958 as an Association of banks dealing in foreign exchange in India (typically called Authorised Dealers - ADs) as a self-regulatory body and is incorporated under Section 25 of The Companies Act, 1956." **The Trap:** Section 25 of the Companies Act, 1956 relates to non-profit companies — appropriate for a trade association like FEDAI. Section 18A of SC(R)A is about derivative contract validity, not FEDAI's incorporation. **Cross-Exam Context:** FEDAI is the self-regulatory body for banks dealing in forex (Authorised Dealers). Its functions include framing rules for inter-bank forex business, accrediting forex brokers, and announcing daily rates. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** FEDAI 1958 + Section 25 Companies Act = not-for-profit self-regulatory body for the AD community.
